title: Physical energy conditions
description: Inventory, supply, consumption, and refinery utilization provide distinct evidence about physical oil-market tightness.
plainLanguageSummary: Inventory, supply, consumption, and refinery utilization provide distinct evidence about physical oil-market tightness.
howToRead: Compare direction, turning points, and sustained divergences. Use the tooltip for exact values and observation dates; visual alignment alone does not establish causation.

calculation.formula: Inventory tightness = -CI_zscore, where CI_zscore uses the prior five-year same-month inventory history; growth rates are year-over-year; refinery utilization is the published rate.
calculation.explanation: The raw surplus series remains available. No single physical indicator defines tightness, and demand weakness can raise inventories even when supply is constrained.
calculation.example: A value at date t uses only observations dated t or earlier unless the chart explicitly labels a visual shift.

patternsToWatch: Turning points or divergences that repeat across historical episodes
limitations: The raw surplus series remains available. No single physical indicator defines tightness, and demand weakness can raise inventories even when supply is constrained.

methodology.formula: Inventory tightness = -CI_zscore, where CI_zscore uses the prior five-year same-month inventory history; growth rates are year-over-year; refinery utilization is the published rate.
methodology.notes: The raw surplus series remains available. No single physical indicator defines tightness, and demand weakness can raise inventories even when supply is constrained.
